fields.name: Kinetic Aqueous Solubility (Nephelometry)
fields.description: Measures kinetic aqueous solubility by adding a DMSO stock solution to aqueous buffer and detecting precipitation by nephelometry. Provides a rapid screen for solubility at pH 7.4.

fields.name: Thermodynamic Solubility (HPLC)
fields.description: Equilibrium solubility determined by saturated shake-flask method followed by HPLC quantification. More accurate than nephelometry; typically run at pH 6.8 and 7.4.

fields.name: PAMPA Permeability (pH 7.4)
fields.description: Parallel Artificial Membrane Permeability Assay. Measures passive transcellular permeability across a hexadecane/phospholipid membrane. High-throughput surrogate for intestinal absorption.

fields.name: Caco-2 Bidirectional Permeability
fields.description: Bidirectional transport assay in Caco-2 cell monolayers measuring apical-to-basolateral (A→B) and basolateral-to-apical (B→A) permeability. Efflux ratio identifies P-gp substrates.

fields.name: Human Liver Microsomal Stability (HLM)
fields.description: Incubation with pooled human liver microsomes and NADPH cofactor. Measures intrinsic clearance (CLint) and half-life to predict hepatic first-pass metabolism.

fields.name: Rat Liver Microsomal Stability (RLM)
fields.description: Metabolic stability assay using pooled rat liver microsomes. Provides species comparison data alongside HLM to support in vivo rat PK study design.

fields.name: Hepatocyte Intrinsic Clearance
fields.description: Suspension hepatocyte stability assay providing whole-cell metabolic clearance, capturing both Phase I (CYP) and Phase II (UGT, SULT) metabolism.

fields.name: Plasma Protein Binding (Rapid Equilibrium Dialysis)
fields.description: Measures fraction unbound (fu) in human and/or rat plasma using rapid equilibrium dialysis (RED). Key parameter for free drug hypothesis and PK/PD modelling.

fields.name: P-glycoprotein Efflux (LLC-PK1-MDR1)
fields.description: Bidirectional transport in MDR1-transfected LLC-PK1 cells. Efflux ratio > 2 indicates P-gp substrate; used to predict CNS penetration and oral bioavailability limitations.

fields.name: BCRP Efflux (MDCK-BCRP)
fields.description: Bidirectional transport in BCRP-transfected MDCK cells. Identifies breast cancer resistance protein substrates which may limit absorption and CNS penetration.
